name: Enpass
description: Offline password manager that stores vaults locally or in your own cloud. One-time purchase option available.

name: NordPass
description: Password manager from the makers of NordVPN. Zero-knowledge encryption, breach scanner, and passkey support.

name: LastPass
description: Cloud-based password manager with autofill, secure sharing, and emergency access. Browser-first approach.

name: Proton Pass
description: End-to-end encrypted password manager from Proton. Includes email alias generation and integrated 2FA.

name: Keeper
description: Enterprise-grade password manager with zero-knowledge security, dark web monitoring, and privileged access management.

name: RoboForm
description: Password manager specializing in form-filling automation. Bookmark-style logins, secure sharing, and offline access.

name: Zoho Vault
description: Password manager for teams and enterprises. Role-based sharing, SSO integration, audit trails, and emergency access.

name: Passbolt
description: Open-source password manager built for teams. Self-hosted with role-based access, sharing, and browser extension.

name: Psono
description: Open-source password manager for teams with client-side encryption. Self-hosted, supports file storage and link sharing.

name: Strongbox
description: KeePass-compatible password manager for Apple platforms. Supports AutoFill, FaceID/TouchID, and iCloud sync with KDBX format.

name: MEGA
description: End-to-end encrypted cloud storage with 20GB free. Zero-knowledge encryption, file sharing, and chat built in.

name: Tresorit
description: End-to-end encrypted cloud storage designed for businesses. Swiss-hosted with zero-knowledge privacy and compliance features.

name: pCloud
description: Cloud storage with lifetime plan option. Client-side encryption add-on, media player, and automatic camera backup.

name: Internxt
description: Open-source, privacy-focused cloud storage with end-to-end encryption. Distributed architecture with zero-knowledge access.

name: Nextcloud
description: Self-hosted cloud storage and collaboration platform. File sync, calendars, contacts, and office document editing on your own server.

name: Filen
description: Zero-knowledge end-to-end encrypted cloud storage. Open-source clients, fast uploads, and real-time file sync.

name: SpiderOak ONE
description: Zero-knowledge encrypted cloud backup and sync. No-knowledge privacy policy means SpiderOak cannot access your data.

name: Auth0
description: Identity platform for authentication and authorization. Supports social login, MFA, and custom rules. Now part of Okta.

name: Clerk
description: Drop-in authentication and user management for modern web apps. Pre-built UI components, session management, and MFA.

name: Keycloak
description: Open-source identity and access management. Supports OIDC, SAML, LDAP federation, and fine-grained authorization.

name: Firebase Auth
description: Google's authentication service supporting email, phone, social, and anonymous sign-in with easy SDK integration.

name: NextAuth.js
description: Open-source authentication library for Next.js. Supports OAuth, credentials, magic links, and database sessions.

name: Okta
description: Enterprise identity platform for workforce and customer identity. SSO, MFA, lifecycle management, and directory integration.

name: SuperTokens
description: Open-source authentication with pre-built login UI. Supports email, social, passwordless, and session management.

name: Zitadel
description: Open-source identity management platform with built-in multi-tenancy, OIDC, SAML, and event sourcing architecture.

name: WorkOS
description: APIs for enterprise-ready auth features. SSO, directory sync, SCIM provisioning, and admin portal for B2B SaaS apps.

name: Stytch
description: Passwordless authentication API. Email magic links, OTPs, OAuth, biometrics, and session management for web and mobile.

name: FusionAuth
description: Self-hosted or cloud identity platform. OAuth2, SAML, passwordless login, consent management, and themeable login pages.

name: Ory
description: Open-source identity infrastructure. Modular components for authentication (Kratos), authorization (Keto), and API gateway (Oathkeeper).

name: Authelia
description: Open-source authentication and authorization server. Provides SSO and 2FA as a companion to reverse proxies like Nginx.

name: HashiCorp Vault
description: Secrets management and data encryption platform. Dynamic secrets, leasing, key rotation, and audit logging.

name: SOPS
description: Mozilla's editor for encrypted files. Encrypts values in YAML, JSON, ENV, and INI files while keeping keys in plaintext.

name: age
description: Simple, modern file encryption tool. No configuration needed, supports recipient-based and passphrase encryption.

name: Doppler
description: Universal secrets manager for teams. Centralized dashboard, environment syncing, and integrations with CI/CD and cloud platforms.

name: AWS Secrets Manager
description: AWS service for managing, rotating, and retrieving secrets like database credentials, API keys, and tokens.

name: 1Password CLI
description: Command-line tool to access 1Password vaults. Inject secrets into scripts, CI/CD pipelines, and environment variables.

name: Infisical
description: Open-source secret management platform. Centralized dashboard, automatic secret rotation, and native CI/CD integrations.

name: Conjur
description: Open-source secrets management by CyberArk. Role-based access, automatic rotation, and audit logging for DevOps pipelines.

name: Akeyless
description: SaaS-based secrets management with zero-knowledge encryption. Dynamic secrets, certificate management, and SSH access.

name: Google Secret Manager
description: GCP service for storing and managing API keys, passwords, and certificates. Automatic rotation, IAM-based access control, and audit logging.

name: Azure Key Vault
description: Microsoft Azure service for safeguarding cryptographic keys, secrets, and certificates. HSM-backed with centralized access policies.

name: Sealed Secrets
description: Kubernetes controller and CLI for encrypting secrets that are safe to store in Git. Only the cluster controller can decrypt them.

name: External Secrets Operator
description: Kubernetes operator that syncs secrets from external APIs like Vault, AWS, and GCP into native Kubernetes Secrets automatically.

name: Teller
description: Universal CLI for accessing secrets from multiple providers. Populates environment variables and detects hardcoded secrets in codebases.

name: Chamber
description: CLI tool for managing secrets via AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store. Reads, writes, and exports secrets as environment variables.

name: git-crypt
description: Transparent encryption for files in a Git repo. Selected files are encrypted on commit and decrypted on checkout using GPG or shared keys.

name: Dotenv Vault
description: Secure sync and management for .env files. Encrypts environment variables with AES-GCM and provides team collaboration features.

name: CyberArk PAM
description: Enterprise privileged access management platform. Vault-based credential storage, session recording, and automated password rotation for critical systems.

name: 1Password Secrets Automation
description: Programmatic access to 1Password vaults via Connect Server. REST API and SDKs for injecting secrets into apps and infrastructure.

name: Delinea Secret Server
description: Enterprise vault for storing and managing privileged credentials. Discovery, rotation, and session monitoring for IT infrastructure secrets.

name: BeyondTrust Password Safe
description: Automated privileged password and session management. Discovers, manages, and audits privileged credentials across the enterprise.

name: Secretive
description: macOS app that stores SSH keys in the Secure Enclave. Keys never leave the hardware, shown as smart card identities to SSH.

name: HCP Vault Secrets
description: HashiCorp Cloud-managed secrets service. Centralized secrets with dynamic credentials, sync to AWS/GCP/Azure, and no infrastructure to manage.

name: Berglas
description: CLI tool for managing secrets on Google Cloud. Wraps Secret Manager and Cloud KMS with create, access, update, and delete commands.

name: Snyk
description: Developer security platform that finds and fixes vulnerabilities in code, dependencies, containers, and infrastructure as code.

name: Trivy
description: Comprehensive open-source security scanner for containers, filesystems, Git repos, and Kubernetes clusters.

name: OWASP ZAP
description: Open-source web application security scanner. Automated and manual testing for finding vulnerabilities in web apps.

name: Dependabot
description: Automated dependency update tool built into GitHub. Creates pull requests to keep dependencies secure and up-to-date.

name: Renovate
description: Automated dependency update tool supporting 90+ package managers. Highly configurable with automerge and grouping.

name: GitGuardian
description: Scans Git repositories for hardcoded secrets, API keys, and credentials. Real-time alerts and remediation workflows.

name: Semgrep
description: Lightweight static analysis tool for finding bugs and enforcing code standards. Supports 30+ languages with custom rules.

name: SonarQube
description: Code quality and security analysis platform. Detects bugs, vulnerabilities, and code smells across 30+ languages.

name: Falco
description: Cloud-native runtime security tool by the CNCF. Detects threats in containers, Kubernetes, and Linux hosts using system call monitoring.

name: Checkov
description: Static analysis tool for infrastructure-as-code. Scans Terraform, CloudFormation, Kubernetes, and Dockerfiles for misconfigurations.

name: Clair
description: Open-source vulnerability scanner for container images. Indexes container layers and matches against known CVE databases.

name: Grype
description: Vulnerability scanner for container images and filesystems by Anchore. Scans SBOMs and supports multiple package ecosystems.

name: Nuclei
description: Fast, template-based vulnerability scanner by ProjectDiscovery. YAML templates for detecting CVEs, misconfigurations, and exposures.

name: Bandit
description: Security linter for Python code. Scans ASTs to find common security issues like hardcoded passwords, SQL injection, and insecure imports.

name: gosec
description: Go security checker that inspects source code AST and SSA for vulnerabilities. Covers injection, crypto, file handling, and taint analysis.

name: Gitleaks
description: Detects hardcoded secrets like API keys, passwords, and tokens in Git repos and files. Regex and entropy-based scanning with pre-commit support.

name: kube-bench
description: Checks Kubernetes clusters against CIS Benchmark security standards. YAML-configurable tests that run as a pod or standalone binary.

name: Syft
description: SBOM generator for container images and filesystems by Anchore. Creates software bills of materials in SPDX, CycloneDX, and other formats.

name: GnuPG
description: Free implementation of the OpenPGP standard. Encrypts and signs data and communications. The backbone of email encryption.

name: VeraCrypt
description: Free disk encryption software based on TrueCrypt. Creates encrypted volumes and encrypts entire partitions or drives.

name: Tor Browser
description: Privacy-focused browser that routes traffic through the Tor network. Prevents tracking, surveillance, and censorship.

name: Tails
description: Portable Linux OS that routes all traffic through Tor. Boots from USB, leaves no trace on the host computer.

name: Cryptomator
description: Transparent client-side encryption for cloud storage. Creates encrypted vaults that work with any cloud sync service.

name: Signal Protocol
description: End-to-end encryption protocol used by Signal, WhatsApp, and others. Double Ratchet algorithm with forward secrecy.

name: Pi-hole
description: Network-wide ad and tracker blocker. Acts as a DNS sinkhole to block ads on all devices without client-side software.

name: Little Snitch
description: macOS firewall that monitors and controls outgoing network connections. Shows which apps communicate with the internet.

name: OpenSnitch
description: GNU/Linux interactive application firewall inspired by Little Snitch. Monitors and controls outbound connections per process.

name: AdGuard Home
description: Network-wide ad and tracker blocking DNS server. Encrypted DNS support (DoH, DoT, DNSCrypt) and parental controls.

name: PrivateBin
description: Zero-knowledge encrypted pastebin. Server has no access to paste content. Self-hosted with burn-after-reading option.
